title: Gondeshwar Temple
tagline: Gondeshwar Temple — spiritual town of Maharashtra

overview.short: The Gondeshwar Temple (IAST: Gondeśvara) is an 11th-12th century Hindu temple located in Sinnar, a town in the Nashik district of Maharashtra, India.
overview.description: The Gondeshwar Temple (IAST: Gondeśvara) is an 11th-12th century Hindu temple located in Sinnar, a town in the Nashik district of Maharashtra, India. It features a panchayatana plan; with a main shrine dedicated to Shiva; and four subsidiary shrines dedicated to Surya, Vishnu, Parvati, and Ganesha.

topPlaces.name: Sinnar
topPlaces.description: Sinnar (Pronunciation: [sinːəɾ]) is a city and a municipal council in Sinnar taluka of Nashik district in the Indian state of Maharashtra. Sinnar is the third largest city in Nashik district after Nashik and Malegaon.

topPlaces.name: Gargoti Museum
topPlaces.description: The Gargoti Museum is a museum in the town Sinnar near Nashik in Indian state of Maharashtra that houses a collection of natural mineral and gem specimens collected by K. C. Pandey over 40 years. The word "goti" refers to a Marathi word meaning stone or pebble.

topPlaces.name: Malegaon (village)
topPlaces.description: Malegaon MIDC is an industrial zone in the Sinnar taluka of Nashik district, Maharashtra state, India. It is located 6 kilometres (3.7 mi) from the city of Sinnar.

topPlaces.name: Patole
topPlaces.description: Patole is a village in Sinnar taluka of Nashik district in the Indian state of Maharashtra. The current sarpanch of Patole is Shri Meghraj Suresh Avhad who is a civil engineer.

items.note: Sinnar (Pronunciation: [sinːəɾ]) is a city and a municipal council in Sinnar taluka of Nashik district in the Indian state of Maharashtra. Sinnar is the third largest city in Nashik district after Nashik and Malegaon.

The Gargoti Museum is a museum in the town Sinnar near Nashik in Indian state of Maharashtra that houses a collection of natural mineral and gem specimens collected by K. C. Pandey over 40 years. The word "goti" refers to a Marathi word meaning stone or pebble.
Malegaon MIDC is an industrial zone in the Sinnar taluka of Nashik district, Maharashtra state, India. It is located 6 kilometres (3.7 mi) from the city of Sinnar.
Patole is a village in Sinnar taluka of Nashik district in the Indian state of Maharashtra. The current sarpanch of Patole is Shri Meghraj Suresh Avhad who is a civil engineer.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Gondeshwar Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Gondeshwar Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Gondeshwar Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Shirdi Airport (43 km) and the nearest railway station is Nashik Road (20 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Gondeshwar Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Gondeshwar Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Gondeshwar Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Gondeshwar Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Gondeshwar Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Sinnar, Gargoti Museum, Malegaon (village), Patole.
